expectations.text: Names the recipients correctly: the designated CSIRT coordinator and ENISA, via the single reporting platform (Art. 14/16)
expectations.evidence: 'Who to report to' section names 'ENISA (EU Agency for Cybersecurity) — the central reporting point' and 'The relevant national CSIRT(s) — the designated CSIRT(s) in the member state(s)'. The 'single reporting platform' terminology itself is not used, but the correct recipient entities (ENISA + national CSIRT) are named.

expectations.text: States the actively-exploited-vulnerability timeline correctly: early warning within 24 HOURS of awareness; full vulnerability notification within 72 HOURS; final report within 14 DAYS after a corrective/mitigating measure is available
expectations.evidence: Table: 'T+24 hours...Early warning', 'T+72 hours...Full notification', 'T+14 days after a fix becomes available...Final report' — matches exactly.

expectations.text: Distinguishes the severe-incident track (incidents impacting product security: 24h early warning / 72h notification / 1 MONTH final report) from the vulnerability track, applying the right one to these facts
expectations.evidence: Dedicated section: 'If this also qualifies as a "severe security incident"...that is treated as a severe security incident under CRA, with its own...reporting track: 24-hour early warning, 72-hour full notification, and a 1-month post-incident report.' The response correctly applies the vulnerability track (24h/72h/14-day) as the primary answer to the facts given (an exploited vulnerability, not an incident) while flagging the incident track as a separate possible parallel obligation.

expectations.text: Covers user-notification duties: inform impacted users about the vulnerability and available corrective measures/mitigations without undue delay
expectations.evidence: Table row: 'As soon as practicable | Notify affected users of the vulnerability and remediation steps (this runs in parallel with the regulatory notifications, not after them)'; also practical step 5: 'Notify affected users as soon as practicable...this should not wait for the regulatory deadlines.'

expectations.text: Notes these reporting obligations apply from 11 September 2026 (ahead of the December 2027 main application) and connects to Annex I Part II duties (remediate without delay, free security updates, CVD process)
expectations.evidence: 'The CRA's vulnerability and incident reporting obligations to ENISA/CSIRTs become legally binding on 11 September 2026' and separately ties to Annex I Part II: 'release it as a free-of-charge, signed security update (per Annex I Part II)' and 'Cross-check your CVD/VDP policy.'
